steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a logical chain: Scottish dialects emphasize the ‘r’ sound, Upland South dialects do the same, and the Upland South was colonized by people of Scottish descent. The blank must complete the conclusion linguists drew from these facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the English dialects spoken in the Upland South acquired their emphasis on the “r” sound from dialects spoken in Scotland.': Follows directly from the colonization history — Scottish settlers brought the feature to the Upland South. ✓
  • Choice B — 'emphasis on the “r” sound will eventually spread from English dialects spoken in the Upland South to dialects spoken elsewhere.': The text gives no basis for predicting future spread. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the English dialects spoken in Scotland were influenced by dialects spoken in the Upland South.': Reverses the causal direction; Scotland influenced the Upland South, not the other way around. ✗
  • Choice D — 'people from Scotland abandoned their emphasis on the “r” sound after relocating to the Upland South.': Contradicts the text, which says the Upland South dialects retained that emphasis. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
